=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (8:41-8:66) TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
  Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file"

[D2] (9:42-9:78) TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
  Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file"

[D3] (15:42-15:64) TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
  Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file"

[D4] (27:32-29:3) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D5] (28:29-28:61) TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
  Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file"

[D6] (32:35-34:3) TS377017: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement. effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body"

[D7] (33:10-33:15) TS377019: This `yield* Effect.fail(...)` passes a yieldable error value. `yield*` represents that value directly without wrapping it in `Effect.fail`. effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)
  Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryFailYieldableError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryFailYieldableError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Replace yield* Effect.fail with yield*"

[D8] (38:36-38:68) TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
  Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file"

[D9] (48:25-48:60) TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
  Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file" ===

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file" ===

=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/globalErrorInEffectFailure.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import { Data, Effect } from "effect"

class CustomError extends Data.TaggedError("CustomError")<{}> {}

export const shouldNotReport = Effect.fail(new CustomError())

export const shouldReport = Effect.fail(new Error("global error"))
export const shouldReport2 = Effect.fail(new globalThis.Error("global error"))

export class BaseExtendsError extends Error {
}

// this is not fine because it extends the global Error type without changing it
export const shouldReport3 = Effect.fail(new BaseExtendsError())

export class BaseExtendsErrorWithTag extends Error {
  readonly _tag = "BaseExtendsErrorWithTag"
}

// this is fine because it has some property that is not the global Error type
export const shouldNotReport2 = Effect.fail(new BaseExtendsErrorWithTag())

// Additional test cases for Effect.gen with global Error

// should report - global Error in Effect.gen
export const shouldReportGen = Effect.fail(new Error("global error in gen"))

// should not report - custom error in Effect.gen
export const shouldNotReportGen = Effect.gen(function* () {
  return yield* Effect.fail(new CustomError())
})

// should report - global Error in Effect.map
export const shouldReportMap = Effect.succeed(1).pipe(
  Effect.flatMap(() => Effect.fail(new Error("global error in map")))
)

// should not report - new Error outside of Effect context
export function shouldNotReportNonEffect() {
  throw new Error("this is not in an Effect")
}

// should report - mapError converting to global Error
export const shouldReportMapError = Effect.fail(new CustomError()).pipe(
  Effect.mapError(() => new Error("mapped to global error"))
)


=== [D5] Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D5] Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryEffectGen for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 2: "Remove the Effect.gen, and keep the body" ===

--- file:///.src/globalErrorInEffectFailure.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import { Data, Effect } from "effect"

class CustomError extends Data.TaggedError("CustomError")<{}> {}

export const shouldNotReport = Effect.fail(new CustomError())

export const shouldReport = Effect.fail(new Error("global error"))
export const shouldReport2 = Effect.fail(new globalThis.Error("global error"))

export class BaseExtendsError extends Error {
}

// this is not fine because it extends the global Error type without changing it
export const shouldReport3 = Effect.fail(new BaseExtendsError())

export class BaseExtendsErrorWithTag extends Error {
  readonly _tag = "BaseExtendsErrorWithTag"
}

// this is fine because it has some property that is not the global Error type
export const shouldNotReport2 = Effect.fail(new BaseExtendsErrorWithTag())

// Additional test cases for Effect.gen with global Error

// should report - global Error in Effect.gen
export const shouldReportGen = Effect.gen(function* () {
  return yield* Effect.fail(new Error("global error in gen"))
})

// should not report - custom error in Effect.gen
export const shouldNotReportGen = Effect.fail(new CustomError())

// should report - global Error in Effect.map
export const shouldReportMap = Effect.succeed(1).pipe(
  Effect.flatMap(() => Effect.fail(new Error("global error in map")))
)

// should not report - new Error outside of Effect context
export function shouldNotReportNonEffect() {
  throw new Error("this is not in an Effect")
}

// should report - mapError converting to global Error
export const shouldReportMapError = Effect.fail(new CustomError()).pipe(
  Effect.mapError(() => new Error("mapped to global error"))
)


=== [D7] Fix 0: "Disable unnecessaryFailYieldableError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D7] Fix 1: "Disable unnecessaryFailYieldableError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D7] Fix 2: "Replace yield* Effect.fail with yield*" ===

--- file:///.src/globalErrorInEffectFailure.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import { Data, Effect } from "effect"

class CustomError extends Data.TaggedError("CustomError")<{}> {}

export const shouldNotReport = Effect.fail(new CustomError())

export const shouldReport = Effect.fail(new Error("global error"))
export const shouldReport2 = Effect.fail(new globalThis.Error("global error"))

export class BaseExtendsError extends Error {
}

// this is not fine because it extends the global Error type without changing it
export const shouldReport3 = Effect.fail(new BaseExtendsError())

export class BaseExtendsErrorWithTag extends Error {
  readonly _tag = "BaseExtendsErrorWithTag"
}

// this is fine because it has some property that is not the global Error type
export const shouldNotReport2 = Effect.fail(new BaseExtendsErrorWithTag())

// Additional test cases for Effect.gen with global Error

// should report - global Error in Effect.gen
export const shouldReportGen = Effect.gen(function* () {
  return yield* Effect.fail(new Error("global error in gen"))
})

// should not report - custom error in Effect.gen
export const shouldNotReportGen = Effect.gen(function* () {
  return yield*new CustomError()
})

// should report - global Error in Effect.map
export const shouldReportMap = Effect.succeed(1).pipe(
  Effect.flatMap(() => Effect.fail(new Error("global error in map")))
)

// should not report - new Error outside of Effect context
export function shouldNotReportNonEffect() {
  throw new Error("this is not in an Effect")
}

// should report - mapError converting to global Error
export const shouldReportMapError = Effect.fail(new CustomError()).pipe(
  Effect.mapError(() => new Error("mapped to global error"))
)


=== [D8] Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D8] Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D9] Fix 0: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D9] Fix 1: "Disable globalErrorInEffectFailure for entire file" ===
skipped by default
